summary: Recent updates to the testing framework in Firefox have focused on improving the reliability and coverage of media-related functionalities, particularly with formats like HEVC (High Efficiency Video Coding) and H.264. Notably, there is now a **higher timeout for launching GMP (Gecko Media Plugins)**, which addresses issues that previously caused tests to fail on Windows systems. Additional enhancements include tests for validating chroma dimensions and stride mismatches in video data inputs, ensuring that media processing is robust and adheres to required specifications. These changes are significant because they help ensure a more stable and efficient handling of media content in the browser, especially as high-quality video streaming becomes increasingly prevalent.
